findings.claim_text: The map is explicit: F: C³ → C³, built from degree-7 polynomials, with Jacobian determinant identically equal to −2 — a nonzero constant, exactly satisfying the conjecture's hypothesis [^2][^3]. And yet three distinct points feed into the same output: F(0, 0, −1/4), F(1, −3/2, 13/2), and F(−1, 3/2, 13/2) all map to (−1/4, 0, 0) [^2][^3].

The counterexample as posted also only addresses dimension three and above — the classical n = 2 case, which is arguably the version of the conjecture mathematicians cared most about, remains open, a distinction the next section takes up directly.
As of July 22, 2026 — more than 48 hours after the Jacobian conjecture story went viral — extensive searching turns up no official Anthropic acknowledgment of the result at all [^17].
